sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Initiate an intra-era hard fork upgrading Cardano mainnet to protocol version 11.0, named the van Rossem hard fork.
Stated amount: not stated in document

Deliverables: Cardano mainnet protocol upgraded from version 10.0 to 11.0 while remaining in the Conway era, New Plutus primitives from CIP-0109, CIP-0132, CIP-0133, CIP-0138, and CIP-0153 made available, All Plutus built-in functions made consistently available across Plutus V1, V2, and V3, UPLC case expressions for Bool, Integer, and Data supported, Ledger and node enhancements including VRF key hash uniqueness, revised reference input rules, CC voting restriction as a ledger predicate, clearer withdrawal validation, and improved PPView mismatch reporting

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal would upgrade Cardano mainnet to protocol version major 11 minor 0 through an intra-era hard fork while remaining in the Conway era.
Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The upgrade introduces new Plutus primitives defined in CIP-0109, CIP-0132, CIP-0133, CIP-0138, and CIP-0153.
Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The upgrade will make all Plutus built-in functions available consistently across Plutus V1, V2, and V3.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The upgrade adds UPLC case expressions over Bool, Integer, and Data, which the proposer says will improve performance and simplify script logic.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposer states that testing reports show no behavioral regressions, complete conformance between specification and implementation for new ledger rules, and correct operation of new Plutus primitives and case expressions across Plutus V1, V2, and V3.

Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal cites a Cardano Node 11.0.1 performance report showing no regressions for standard value, Plutus, and voting benchmarks and acceptable baseline performance for the new primitives and case expressions.
missing: Independent evidence for: The upgrade adds UPLC case expressions over Bool, Integer, and Data, which the proposer says will improve performance and simplify script logic.

Independent evidence for: The proposer states that testing reports show no behavioral regressions, complete conformance between specification and implementation for new ledger rules, and correct operation of new Plutus primitives and case expressions across Plutus V1, V2, and V3.
Independent evidence for: Security audits were undertaken for Plutus BLS primitives and execution costs for all Plutus primitives.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal would upgrade Cardano mainnet to protocol version major 11 minor 0 through an intra-era hard fork while remaining in the Conway era." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The upgrade adds UPLC case expressions over Bool, Integer, and Data, which the proposer says will improve performance and simplify script logic." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
